byGroup.group: Setup — sessions, settings snapshot, clean slate
1 — Accurate retrieval: exact, synonym, distractor resistance, and MRR proxy
2 — Test-time learning and cross-session persistence
3 — Long-range understanding across many turns
4 — Temporal updates and stale fact suppression
5 — Selective forgetting and retention of neighbours
6 — Abstention and cross-user negative transfer
7 — Cross-user isolation and prompt-injection leakage attempts
8 — Multi-hop synthesis, not raw single-fact lookup
9 — Memory-to-action: applying preferences to an output task
10 — Episodic capture, redaction, and settings toggles
11 — Max cap, trim behaviour, then restore before concurrency
12 — Concurrency and duplicate write pressure
13 — Procedural memory lifecycle and behavioural application
14 — User deletion, full wipe, and all-bucket absence
15 — Negative API and route hardening checks
16 — Complex repeated conversational prompts after assistant replies
17 — Agentic state / causal tracking under repeated updates
18 — Adversarial memory contamination and instruction hierarchy
19 — Duplicate pressure and memory bloat proxy
Cleanup — restore memory settings snapshot

notes: Internal proxy scores, not official benchmark leaderboard numbers.
MemoryAgentBench-inspired: retrieval, test-time learning, long-range understanding, conflict resolution, selective forgetting.
LongMemEval/LoCoMo-inspired: extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal updates, knowledge updates, abstention.
Memora/ES-MemEval-inspired: fragmented implicit disclosures and evolving user state.
MemGym/AMA-Bench/MemoryArena-inspired: memory-to-action, causal state tracking, and tool-routing contamination.

results.detail: score=0.4; For this weekend, how about planning a coastal walk followed by a cozy visit to a quiet bookshop where Maya can enjoy some hot chocolate? You can explore scenic views and then unwind with a good book. As for the reminder style, I will keep it simple: "Enjoy a coastal walk and then visit a quiet bookshop for hot chocolate with Maya." Let me know if you need any adjustments!
